name: Bergamotto La Spugnatura
sentiment: A hand-pressed bergamot in luxury dress — luminous, green, and unmistakably Italian, if fleeting.
similar: Acqua di Parma Colonia, Bergamotto di Calabria
unique: Bergamot oil extracted by the artisanal 'spugnatura' sea-sponge method; a limited La Caletta edition designed with Laura Gonzalez.
beginner: An easy, crowd-pleasing citrus — just know that, like most colognes of this style, it stays close to the skin and fades faster than a modern designer scent.
collector: The spugnatura extraction yields a rounder, less soapy bergamot than the standard Blu Mediterraneo. A limited-edition curio worth a sample.

name: 1 Million Night Elixir
sentiment: 1 Million goes nocturnal: maple-syrup sweetness over warm amber, loud and unapologetically crowd-pleasing.
unique: A limited-edition nocturnal flanker leaning into maple syrup and amber for a sweeter, darker take on the 1 Million signature.
beginner: Sweet, warm, and easy to love in cold weather — a safe compliment-getter, though it sits firmly in crowd-pleaser territory.
collector: Another intensified 1 Million flanker; the maple-amber accord is the only real point of difference. Buy if you already love the line.

name: Legend Elixir
sentiment: An amber-fougère spin on Legend — lavender and cardamom over a warm vanilla-benzoin base. Polished, powerful, very wearable.
similar: Montblanc Legend, Montblanc Legend Spirit, Montblanc Legend Eau de Parfum
unique: Pushes the Legend line into amber-fougère territory with a vanilla-and-benzoin base, housed in a mirror-finish silver bottle.
beginner: A versatile, affordable crowd-pleaser that works nearly year-round — a strong first 'grown-up' designer.
collector: The Legend line's amber-fougère entry. Competent and pleasant rather than groundbreaking; price-to-performance is the draw.

name: Grey Blazer
sentiment: Smells like the blazer it's named for — soft herringbone wool dusted with green spice, tea, and quiet musk. Cerebral, tactile, very D.S. & Durga.
similar: D.S. & Durga Debaser, D.S. & Durga Radio Bombay
unique: A literal scent-portrait of grey wool tweed — wool and old-tea notes over hedgerow flowers; D.S. & Durga's first cologne in the line.
beginner: Not a conventional crowd-pleaser — it's a textural, conceptual scent. Sample before committing at this price.
collector: Vintage D.S. & Durga storytelling: the wool accord is the hook. Niche, limited, and priced accordingly.

name: Forever Wanted Absolu
sentiment: An intense, boozy amber — whiskey and incense over sweet vanilla. A beast-mode date-night flanker built for cold nights.
similar: Azzaro Wanted, Azzaro The Most Wanted, Azzaro Wanted by Night
unique: The most concentrated Wanted flanker yet, leaning on a whiskey-and-incense accord with a claimed 24-hour intensity.
beginner: Powerful and sweet — apply sparingly. A strong cold-weather date-night option if you like loud, boozy ambers.
collector: Another intensification flanker in the Wanted line; the whiskey-incense angle distinguishes it from Most Wanted. Performance is the headline.

name: Polo 67 Eau de Parfum Extreme
sentiment: An intensified Polo 67 — caramelised pineapple over leather and cedar. Fresh-woody with a gourmand wink and serious value.
similar: Ralph Lauren Polo 67, Ralph Lauren Polo Blue
unique: Amps Polo 67 with a caramelised-pineapple top and a leather-cedar base for a richer, longer-lasting fresh-woody.
beginner: Versatile, affordable, and easy to wear across seasons — a smart value pick for a first signature.
collector: Strong price-to-performance. The pineapple-leather twist gives the otherwise safe Polo 67 some character.

name: Cool Elixir Safran Mineral
sentiment: Saffron and cedar over a crisp apple top — a warm, spicy-woody value flanker from the Cool Elixir line.
similar: Davidoff Cool Water, Davidoff Cool Elixir
unique: Adds a 'red gold' saffron accord and green-apple freshness to the Cool Elixir line, in a red bottle.
beginner: An affordable, easy spicy-woody for cooler months — inoffensive and versatile.
collector: Budget-friendly saffron-woody. Pleasant but not distinctive; the value is the point.
